memoryArtifacts.headline: Life Sketch of Robert Page   B. 1577
memoryArtifacts.description:
Robert was born about 1577 in England.  He was the son of Robert and Martha Page.

     He married Margaret Goodawynge (Goodwin) in July 1598 in Hucklington, Norfolk, England.  Two years later they …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Page II於從1577年1月11日到1578年1月10日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可阿可爾時，他的父親Robert Page35歲，母親Martha32歲。他於1598年7月16日與Margaret Goodwin在英國諾福爾可布林頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1617年7月7日在英國英格蘭諾福爾可，埋葬在英國諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特。

nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

variants.name: Pages
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Luis, Mauricio, Ramon, Fernando, Jose, Lazaro, Pedro, Alfredo.  French Jacques, Lucien, Michel.

Catalan and southern French (Pagès): occupational name for a peasant farmer or agricultural laborer, from Catalan and Old Occitan pagès ‘peasant’ (from Late Latin pagensis, a derivative of pagus ‘village, country district’).
English: variant of Page with genitival of post-medieval excrescent s.

variants.name: Pageau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Stephane, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gisele, Jean Claude, Lucien, Philippe.

variants.name: Lepage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Pierre, Raoul, Adelard, Alcide, Christophe, Lucien, Normand, Adrien.
French: occupational or status name for a young servant, a variant of Page 1, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Lapage .
French Canadian: altered form of Pagé (see Page 2).

variants.name: Padgett
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from a Middle English diminutive of page, paige ‘servant, groom’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Littlepage
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English littel ‘small’ +Middle English, Old French page ‘young servant’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Pashia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Pagé (see Page 2).
History: It was Jean Baptiste Pagé from QC whose surname was changed to Pashia after he settled in MO in the 1810s. Some of his descendants bear a rare variant, Pashea.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Page II was born from 11 January 1577 to 10 January 1578, in Acle, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Page, was 35 and his mother, Martha, was 32. He married Margaret Goodwin on 16 July 1598, in Hemblington, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 7 July 1617, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Page II est né du 11 janvier 1577 au 10 janvier 1578, à Acle, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Page, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Martha, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Goodwin le 16 juillet 1598, à Hemblington, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 7 juillet 1617, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, Angleterre.

ko.St. Michael, Ormesby, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 믹하얼
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Page II(이)가 1577년 1월 11일부터 1578년 1월 10일까지 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 악일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Page(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Martha(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1598년 7월 16일 영국 노르폴크 헤블링턴에서 Margaret Goodwin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1617년 7월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 마거릿에 묻혔다.

ru.St. Michael, Ormesby, Norfolk, England: ормесби ст мачаел, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Page II родился с 11 января 1577 по 10 января 1578 в местности акл, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Page было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Martha, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Goodwin 16 июля 1598 г. в местности хемблингтон, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 7 июля 1617 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности ормесби ст маргарет, норфолк, Англия.
